definition: A gradual 25-50bp ratchet in inflation expectations operating below the Coibion-Gorodnichenko threshold through firm pricing behavior and consumer acceptance, distinct from formal de-anchoring (discontinuous >75bp sustained divergence). Michigan 5Y-10Y expectations at 3.0-3.2% may represent this phenomenon.
evidence: 1966-69 and 2004-07 precedents show drift occurring without triggering aggressive policy response
Michigan 5Y-10Y at 3.0-3.2% potentially exhibits this pattern
Operates through firm pricing behavior and consumer acceptance below policy thresholds
open_questions: Is the drift mechanism sufficiently documented in behavioral finance or survey-based research?
Can drift be distinguished from noise in real-time expectations data?
At what point does drift become self-reinforcing and cross into de-anchoring?
